sections.paragraph:
ἀνάστασις, εως, ἡ [ἀνίστημι]

—1. ‘a bringing to a higher status’, raising up, rising (opp. of πτῶσις) Lk 2:34.

—2. ‘a rising from the condition of being dead’, resurrection (freq. with ἐκ νεϰρῶν)

—a. as a phenomenon per se Mt 22:23 and par.; J 11:24; 1 Cor 15:12f; 2 Ti 2:18; Hb 11:35; misinterpreted as a transcendent entity Ac 17:18; of privileged resurrection Lk 20:35; cp. Ac 4:2 and the various destina-tions of the resurrected J 5:29; of a so-called ‘first’ resurrection Rv 20:5f; of Jesus’ resurrection Ac 1:22.

—b. of Jesus as source of resurrection J 11:25; cp. Ro 6:5.
